title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping · Independent Career Trial
summary: A different candidate, role family, location, access need, employment history, constraint, and hiring process requires an independent transfer of job description decomposition and evidence mapping into a changed role requirement and evidence matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of role requirement and evidence matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
Classify stated requirements as essential evidence, likely preference, negotiable proxy, process constraint, or unresolved question.
Map each priority outcome to direct, adjacent, transferable, missing, or contradictory candidate evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Extraction
steps.instruction:
lab case — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Extraction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Extraction, keep Outcome Extraction connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Outcome Extraction condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub Profile Documentation — Controls profile visibility, optional public identity, pinned work, contribution context, and the personal safety consequences of public disclosure.

At Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Extraction, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Outcome Extraction, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Matching nouns matters more than understanding expected work.

options.text: Matching nouns matters more than understanding expected work. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Outcome Extraction.

For commit to the mission model, apply Outcome Extraction without its stated preconditions, omit the role requirement and evidence matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Outcome Extraction for role requirement and evidence matrix.
State the Outcome Extraction preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

stimulus.title: Go No Go case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. Artifact under review: role requirement and evidence matrix.

options.text: For audit the initial evidence packet, apply Go No Go without its stated preconditions, omit the role requirement and evidence matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Go No Go for role requirement and evidence matrix.
The record names the assumption, applies Go No Go, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Go No Go record says role requirement and evidence matrix looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the initial evidence packet evidence boundary for Go No Go.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Go No Go
steps.instruction:
Append a career-go-no-go-1hesvr: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

content.code:
career-go-no-go-1hesvr:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Go No Go structural check is only one gate. role requirement and evidence matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

stimulus.title: Go No Go failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Go No Go condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Go No Go output and hide the violated role requirement and evidence matrix assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Go No Go.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Go No Go without its stated preconditions, omit the role requirement and evidence matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Go No Go for role requirement and evidence matrix.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Go No Go: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go.
steps.why: Retrieving Go No Go through the concrete role requirement and evidence matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go, keep Go No Go connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Go No Go precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The lab variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Go No Go, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission.
